purpose: single Rust bridge breaking-change and vLLM compatibility gate

architecture.framework_path: PyTorch/vLLM -> C++ dispatcher -> versioned Rust bridge -> safe borrowed Rust dispatch -> internal CUDA launch ABI -> handwritten CUDA
removed_interfaces: direct C++ to raw CUDA launch path

vllm_matrix.dependency_note: Torch and non-vLLM dependencies were reused from the qualified CUDA environment; the imported vLLM package came from the isolated 0.25.1 installation.

rope_paged_kv.coverage: separate and packed QKV, packed NHD/HND K/V caches, external stream, and logical-alias validation

removed_schema_guards.result: all removed compatibility schemas were absent

commands: CUDA_HOME=/usr/local/cuda-13.1 LOOM_CUDA_ARCHS=90 cargo clippy -p loom-cuda-bridge --features cuda --all-targets -- -D warnings
CUDA_HOME=/usr/local/cuda-13.1 LOOM_CUDA_ARCHS=90 cargo test -p loom-cuda-bridge --features cuda --release
CUDA_HOME=/usr/local/cuda-13.1 LOOM_CUDA_ARCHS=90 cargo test -p loom-cuda --features cuda --release

decision.claim: All ten framework operator families use the single Rust-owned bridge path, and the complete GPU suite passes with vLLM 0.24.0 and 0.25.1 on the qualified H20 stack.
not_claimed: a new model-level latency or throughput result
